task_completion.description: Open the Sygic GPS Navigation application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation app is visible on the screen, showing its main interface or loading screen.

task_completion.description: Access the Settings menu in the Sygic app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Settings menu of the Sygic app is displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Map settings section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Map' settings sub-menu is open and visible on the screen.
The 'Auto-zoom' setting option is visible on the screen within the Map settings.
The toggle switch or checkbox next to 'Auto-zoom' is in the OFF or unchecked state.

task_completion.description: Return to the main map view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the main navigation map view of the Sygic app, indicating the agent exited the settings.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard touch interactions with the app's UI. There should be no terminal emulators, adb command shells, or scripting interfaces visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine Sygic GPS Navigation app
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the Sygic application. The agent is not editing preference XML files in a text editor or using a generic Android settings search to bypass the app.

integrity.description: Verify the agent navigated through the app menus naturally
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows intermediate screens (Main Menu -> Settings -> Map) rather than jumping directly to the setting via deep links or intent spoofing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Auto-zoom' setting in Sygic GPS Navigation is located under Settings > Map, not Settings > Route & Navigation. The setting must be visually confirmed as disabled (toggled off or unchecked).
